# v1.0.0-rc.1 — Release Candidate 1

**Released:** 2026-04-29
**Type:** Pre-release
**Audience:** Factory operators upgrading from v0.79.x or v1.0.0-beta.x; plugin authors evaluating v1.0 ABI

## Summary

First release candidate for v1.0.0. Closes the 4-month beta cycle by shipping all remaining v1.0 epics. WASM plugin ecosystem fully wired (4/4 lifecycle events: DRIFT-006 closed). Multi-sink observability (file/HTTP/Datadog/Honeycomb/OTel-grpc with retry/circuit-breaker/DLQ/routing/tag-enrichment/internal.sink_error events) shipped Waves 11+12. Operator migration guide + semver commitment doc shipped Wave 14.

## What changed since v1.0.0-beta.7

### New features

#### Wave 11 — Plugin ecosystem and observability foundations (commits since 2026-04-27)
- **S-3.01/02/03/04 — WASM plugin ports** (PR #20/21/22/23): capture-commit-activity, capture-pr-activity, block-ai-attribution, plus the legacy-bash-adapter compat layer. Cross-platform native plugins (no git-bash dependency on darwin/linux for these three).
- **S-4.09 — sink-http retry/jitter** (PR #27): exponential backoff with full jitter on transient failures.
- **S-4.10 — internal.sink_error events** (PR #28): cross-sink error fan-out for observability of observability.

#### Wave 12 — Sink robustness and rc.1 gate (commits 2026-04-28)
- **S-4.05 — Dead Letter Queue** (PR #29): per-sink retry-exhaustion DLQ writer with byte-counted rotation; clock injection; SinkDlqEvent emission.
- **S-4.06 — Per-sink routing + tag enrichment** (PR #30): RoutingFilter declarative event-type/plugin allow-deny lists; static tags applied to all events fanning into a sink; `internal.event_filtered` counter for silent-drop observability.
- **S-4.07 — E2E observability integration tests** (PR #31): 40 integration tests covering 9 modules; sink-datadog + sink-honeycomb promoted to runtime deps; relay-channel pattern for HoneycombSink.
- **S-4.08 — rc.1 release gate spec** (PR #32): 20 ACs governing rc.1 cut criteria (this release).

#### Wave 13 — Lifecycle hooks (commits 2026-04-28..29; DRIFT-006 closure)
- **S-5.01 — SessionStart hook** (PR #35): fires once per Claude Code session; emits `session.started` with platform/plugin_count/timestamp.
- **S-5.02 — SessionEnd hook** (PR #36): fires once per session; emits `session.ended` with duration_ms/tool_call_count/timestamp.
- **S-5.03 — WorktreeCreate/Remove hooks** (PR #37): single crate handles both events via internal dispatch; emits `worktree.created` / `worktree.removed`.
- **S-5.04 — PostToolUseFailure hook** (PR #38): emits `tool.error` on tool execution failure with tool_name + error_message (UTF-8-safe 1000-char truncation).

#### Wave 14 — v1.0 documentation (commits 2026-04-29)
- **S-5.05 — Migration guide v0.79.x → v1.0** (PR #40): full operator-facing upgrade guide at `docs/guide/migrating-from-0.79.md` (10 sections; 5 troubleshooting issues; rollback procedure).
- **S-5.06 — Semver commitment doc** (PR #39): `docs/guide/semver-commitment.md` documents stable surface (hook-sdk ABI, registry schema, hooks.json format, event type namespaces), unstable surface (internal JSONL, dispatcher CLI args), breaking change policy, plugin backward compat (HOST_ABI_VERSION = 1 frozen).

### Quality and governance

- Wave 14 spec convergence: 11 adversarial passes total (vs Wave 13's 51) — Wave 13 lessons applied up-front compressed convergence ~4x.
- 12 governance policies registered in `.factory/policies.yaml` (baseline 1-9 + project-specific 10-12).
- 1,912 behavioral contracts across 10 subsystems with full L1-L4 traceability.
- Semgrep SAST integrated (workflow at `.github/workflows/semgrep.yml`, image now pinned).
- 12-policy adversarial review framework matured.

## Migration path

Operators on v1.0.0-beta.7 can upgrade with:

```
/plugin update vsdd-factory@vsdd-factory
/vsdd-factory:activate
# Restart Claude Code session
/vsdd-factory:factory-health
```

Operators on v0.79.x: see `docs/guide/migrating-from-0.79.md` (S-5.05) for the full operator migration guide.

## Calendar gate deviations (documented for transparency)

This rc.1 cut deliberately overrides two calendar-gated ACs from S-4.08:

- **AC-9 (BC-9.01.006 PC1)** — 14-day beta-shakedown window from beta.7 (2026-04-26). Strict reading: not satisfied (3 days elapsed at cut). **Rationale for override:** rc.1 IS the shakedown vehicle; a fresh 14-day rc.1 shakedown clock starts at this tag-push for v1.0.0 GA gating. v1.0.0 GA (S-5.07) MUST satisfy the strict shakedown — no further override permitted at GA.
- **AC-10 (BC-9.01.006 PC4)** — 7-day WASM port production exposure (S-3.01/02/03 merged 2026-04-27). Strict reading: not satisfied (2 days elapsed). **Rationale for override:** same shakedown-as-vehicle argument; rc.1 starts the WASM port exposure clock for v1.0.0 GA.

These deviations are recorded in factory-artifacts D-151 (next state-manager seal). v1.0.0 GA (S-5.07) cannot override either gate.

## Other deferred items

- **AC-2** (cargo publish dry-run for vsdd-hook-sdk): deferred to TD-008. `vsdd-hook-sdk-macros` must be published to crates.io before v1.0.0 GA. rc.1 does not publish to crates.io.
- **AC-13** (CHANGELOG monotonicity script): deferred to TD-009. Pre-existing same-day beta.6/beta.7 entries cause the strict-`<` script to fail; not introduced by rc.1.
- **AC-4** (commit.made sustained-load smoke) + **AC-11** (multi-sink dogfood): synthetic best-effort smoke ran during rc.1 prep; full operator dogfood happens during rc.1 shakedown.

## Known regressions

None. (Wave 9 SS-01 straggler S-2.07 regression sweep clean; no P0 issues at tag time.)

## What's next

- **rc.1 shakedown:** 14-day window starts at tag push. P0 issues with `label:beta-shakedown` reset the clock per BC-9.01.006 PC5.
- **v1.0.0 GA (S-5.07):** calendar-gated 1 week post-Wave-14 merge AND fresh 14-day rc.1 shakedown clear (whichever is later); MUST honor strict AC-9/AC-10 — no further overrides permitted.
- **v1.0.1+ candidates:** TD-006/008/009 + 7 MINOR consistency findings (F-006..F-012) from post-Wave-9 sweep.

## Acknowledgements

Built using the vsdd-factory itself (self-referential VSDD pipeline). Thanks to the test-writers, adversaries, implementers, and the discipline they imposed.
